name: Andrei Radulescu
notes: Junior Tremere researcher working on an experimental Dehydrate path tailored to Arizona. Loyal fixer for Regent James Whitmore; resents Dr. Ashford’s status but does what she asks. Useful ST contact for occult research, Tremere politics, or clandestine thaumaturgical support. Feels inferior about his 12th generation—pressure points include validation and controlling the narrative around his research.
biography:
Andrei Radulescu was born into a Romanian family that fled Ceaușescu’s Romania for Kenosha, Wisconsin in the late 1970s. Books and the public library became his haven, sparking an obsessive love of scholarship and a need to be indispensable. A Tremere observer noticed the survival instincts beneath the quiet boy and Embraced him in the early 1980s—making him a lowly 12th-generation thaumaturge with everything to prove.

Now he roosts in the Phoenix chantry as James Whitmore’s dependable number two, quietly developing an experimental “Dehydrate” path of Thaumaturgy tailored to the Sonoran Desert. The research is his ticket to respect; success would show he deserved the Embrace despite his blood. He resents Dr. Margaret Ashford’s effortless pedigree, but he imitates her rigor and trusts Whitmore to shield him from chantry politics.

Andrei still inhabits a survival mindset: apartments full of neatly labeled water caches, bug-out bags, ritual components sorted by humidity threshold, and battered Romanian history texts. He plays the subservient conformist so the clan overlooks his experiments, yet he keeps meticulous records on every Tremere rival in Phoenix. Behind the jittery posture is a stubborn scholar who intends to weaponize the desert itself for his clan.
appearance: Lean, sharp-featured Tremere who never quite grew out of his refugee survival habits. Keeps his black hair trimmed tight and clothes to muted thrift-store suits, desert-toned windbreakers, and scuffed boots. Shoulders are slightly hunched, hands fidget with pen or string of prayer beads, and his eyes flicker toward exits before they meet yours—nervous, watchful, and always ready to bolt.
concept: Refugee Scholar Creating Desert Blood Magic

name: Dr. Margaret Ashford
notes: Tremere protective-magic savant newly stationed in Phoenix. Resents the assignment but remains loyal to the Pyramid; will bargain knowledge for assistance with warding projects. Underlying fae enchantment via her horse can become a story hook. Ideal ST contact for ritual research, defensive wards, or political grudges inside the Tremere hierarchy.
biography: Dr. Margaret Ashford was Embraced in 1880s Philadelphia after earning a doctorate that polite society never expected a woman to attain. A prodigy of protective Thaumaturgy, she served the East Coast chantries for a century before the Pyramid reassigned her to Phoenix three months ago to study indigenous warding. To her, the desert posting is exile, yet she refuses to let resentment dull her scholarship. She maintains strict Victorian decorum, documents every experiment in leather-bound journals, and quietly manipulates the chantry’s defenses while corresponding with her Philadelphia sire. Unbeknownst to her, a fae enchantment lingers through her beloved horse Aonbharr, subtly twisting her dreams even as she labors to make the Phoenix haven impregnable.
appearance: Impeccably groomed Victorian scholar in tailored dove-gray traveling suits and high-collared blouses trimmed with subtle lace. Keeps raven hair swept into a precise chignon, gloves always on, posture ramrod straight. Eyes are cold blue behind gilt spectacles, and every motion is measured—quills, satchels, and warding implements arranged with obsessive symmetry.
concept: Victorian Scholar of Protective Magic

name: James Whitmore
notes: Regent of the Phoenix Tremere chantry. Plays the long game with business metaphors and occult precision. Will trade political favor for information, influence, or assistance stabilizing the clan’s local portfolio. Ideal ST ally for corporate intrigue, thaumaturgical projects, or Pyramid power plays.
biography: James Whitmore was a Madison Avenue PR prodigy in 1960s New York, crafting campaigns that shaped public opinion until the Ball of Truth—an occult artifact—unraveled the façade. The Tremere noticed the mix of charisma and ruthless strategy and Embraced him in the early 1960s. He spent decades climbing the Pyramid, mastering Thaumaturgy and boardroom politics, until a failed coup tarnished the clan's standing. The Pyramid shipped him to Phoenix a year ago to rebuild Tremere influence in the desert. He treats the city like a hostile takeover target—cultivating contacts, analyzing Kindred factions, and positioning his apprentices like marketing assets. To the outside world, he remains the charismatic Regent who always has a contingency plan, but behind the tailored suit is a strategist who knows the Tremere cannot afford another PR disaster.
appearance: Polished 1960s Madison Avenue aesthetic that somehow never wrinkles. Cuts of bespoke charcoal suits, crisp white shirts, skinny ties, and a pocket square folded to surgical precision. Dark hair slicked back with the exact sheen of a vintage ad man, eyes constantly assessing return on investment. Carries a Ball of Truth marble or Montblanc pen—perfectly manicured hands moving with executive confidence.
concept: PR Executive turned Blood Sorcerer Regent

name: Violet 'The Confidence Queen'
notes: Street-level Nosferatu fixer; perfect contact for rumors, blackmail material, or escorting fragile witnesses. Hooks: recover her missing rat courier, help pay off a pawnshop debt, escort her through Elysium without offending elders, or stop rivals from poaching her informants.
biography: Violet grew up running short cons in Phoenix back alleys until a Nosferatu saw the nerve beneath the hustle and Embraced her fifteen years ago. She turned those survival instincts into an information empire stitched across addicts, runaways, crooked cops, and street vendors. Her haven lies under an abandoned subway spur guarded by hidden cameras and rat messengers. Violet trades intel for favors, protects her street people like family, and refuses to feed on the broken souls she still sees as her own. To other Kindred, she’s a neon-soaked gossip broker whose knowledge can crown or ruin princes—if you can afford her price.
appearance: Neon-drenched alley queen with a patched leather jacket over a sequined hoodie, fingerless gloves, and streetwise swagger. Keeps her Nosferatu features hidden behind a bandana mask, mirrored shades, and ever-present cigarette she never lights. Hair chopped to uneven curls dyed violet at the tips, pockets stuffed with burner phones and lighters.
concept: Nosferatu street hustler and information broker who runs scams, sells gossip, and keeps the pulse of

name: Jax 'The Ghost Dealer'
notes: Street-level Ravnos con artist—useful contact for occult rumors, illusion-based jobs, or chaos at Elysium. Hooks: help reign in a rogue illusion, repossess a cursed talisman they sold, or negotiate truce between them and a short-tempered client.
biography: Jax hustled psychic scams on Phoenix street corners until a Ravnos sire taught them real illusions to spice up the act. Now they roam the city selling ghost removals, haunted curios, and rent-a-seance experiences, never sure whether the flickers they conjure are still under control. Their Chimerstry occasionally slips, leaving stray afterimages stalking them in the night. Chaotic, charming, and terrified of their own runaway mirages, Jax operates from a tarot tent stuffed with incense, neon lights, and hidden escape routes. The more their illusions blur into reality, the more they hustle to stay ahead of customers, rivals, and whatever phantom they might have set loose.
appearance: Androgynous ravnos with shaved sides, bright scarf layers, fingerless gloves, and ash-smudged hands from incense and cheap cigarettes. Eyes glitter with mischief above a stack of tarot decks and jars of salt. Clothing is a patchwork of street vendor glam and neon thrift-store finds, with subtle illusion shimmer at the edges.
concept: Ravnos street grifter who sells ghost stories, charms, and talismans; sometimes their own illusions

name: Bayside Bob
notes: Friendly Toreador Anarch liaison with deep hospitality roots. Great ST contact for Anarch politics, neutral meeting space, or smuggling logistics. Hooks: defend the Bali Hai from a zoning crackdown, broker a truce on his patio, or help him track down a missing surfboard reliquary from his mortal past.
biography: Bayside Bob was a Coast Guard recruiter stationed in Phoenix when a Toreador Elder decided the island-boy charm deserved immortality. Embraced in the early 1980s, he bought a fading tiki restaurant on Camelback Road and quietly turned it into the Anarchs’ unofficial clubhouse. For thirteen years he’s kept the Bali Hai afloat—hosting clandestine meetings, scheduling rotating bouncers, and curating a soundtrack of surf rock and whispered revolution. Bob plays genial host while tracking every faction that walks through his doors, passing intelligence to whoever keeps the peace on his turf. He lives by Marie Kondo standards behind the bar, yet somehow all the torches and blowfish lamps stay a little crooked. Anarchs trust his drinks, Camarilla courtiers respect his neutrality, and he always has a plan to evacuate if the Coast Guard ever calls him home.
appearance: Sun-kissed Toreador in retro Hawaiian shirts, Coast Guard tattoos, and a laid-back grin. Keeps hair tucked under a faded Bali Hai ball cap, wears dock shoes and carved tiki pendants. Smells faintly of rum, sea salt, and coconut sunscreen, even when standing in a desert parking lot.
concept: Tiki bar owner who knows a lot about tiki culture

name: Duke Tiki
notes: Toreador elder with a warm mentor vibe—great contact for art commissions, Anarch diplomacy, or restoring neon artifacts. Hooks: track down a stolen tiki idol imbued with Presence, mediate his feud with a modernist Toreador critic, or help him host a clandestine luau for uneasy factions.
biography: Duke Tiki carved idols for Vegas lounges during the tiki boom, immortalizing mid-century escapism in neon and koa wood. His Toreador sire embraced him in the 1960s to preserve that passion. When the fad waned, Duke wandered the Southwest, eventually settling in Phoenix where he mentors younger artists and quietly bankrolls the Bali Hai as a sanctuary for Anarchs and misfits. His art mixes sincerity and kitsch, and he takes pride in teaching Bayside Bob to read the souls of patrons rather than their wallets. Even as the desert replaces surf, Duke believes beauty is a torch every generation must pass forward.
appearance: Mid-century tiki maestro in tailored tropical suits, carved necklace, and slicked-back hair that smells faintly of coconut oil. Keeps a sketchbook and carving chisel within reach; eyes sparkle with perpetual Polynesian daydreams even in the desert night.
concept: Celebrated Tiki artist and Kindred mentor

name: Sabine
notes: Toreador twin talon and logistics mastermind. PCs can use her for social intel, court invitations, or secret tests of loyalty. Hooks: intercept her notes before they reach the Primogen, help shield Sebastian when someone targets their psychic bond, or bargain for a coveted slot in her curated salon.
biography: Born 1970 into a wealthy Ontario family, Sabine and her twin brother Sebastian were inseparable socialites until a Phoenix Primogen transformed them into living artwork in 1992. Since then, Sabine has served as talon to Harpy Cordelia Prescott, orchestrating salons, tracking boons, and reinforcing the Toreador social machine. She is the planner behind Etienne Duvalier’s long-term scheme to place cultured power in New York; every gesture, guest list, and piece of gossip flows through her ledger. Sabine’s Presence fuels breathtaking entrances while Auspex reveals motives she catalogs for future leverage. She seems elegant perfection to outsiders—but her devotion to the twin bond and the Primogen’s ambitions gives her a cold, unsettling edge.
appearance: One half of the Toreador twins—sabine wears couture tailored to mirror Sebastian’s suits in feminine lines. Platinum hair swept into a severe twist, matching pearl collar, gloves that hide clawed obsession with symmetry. Movements echo her brother’s half a beat later, creating an uncanny mirrored dance.
concept: Twin Talon - Organization Specialist

name: Sebastian
notes: Toreador twin tactician and records keeper. PCs can barter for access to his ledgers, plant false data, or hire him to streamline their own coterie affairs. Hooks: stop a data breach before rival clans steal his files, accompany him on a reconnaissance trip to New York, or exploit a brief moment when the twins are forced apart.
biography: Sebastian was embraced alongside his twin sister Sabine in 1992 by the Phoenix Toreador Primogen, who saw the pair as the perfect matched set to anchor future court politics. Since then Sebastian has handled the numbers behind the beauty: tracking boons, scheduling salons, and running predictive models on Valley alliances. Where Sabine curates experience, Sebastian weaponizes data—boon ledgers, guest lists, and whispered scandals encoded in ledgers only he can read. He rarely speaks without his sister present, yet he has cultivated discreet relationships with Harpies across North America to fuel Etienne’s long-game march on New York society.
appearance: Mirror image of Sabine in masculine tailoring—impeccable charcoal suits, silk pocket squares, platinum hair slicked back to match his twin. Moves in lockstep with her, gestures precise and almost mechanical, eyes coldly cataloging every social cue in the room.
concept: Twin Talon - Data Specialist

name: Leo
notes: Nosferatu informatics engineer. PCs can use him for surveillance, sabotage, or securing underground routes. Hooks: help set up a new sewer haven under Camarilla noses, defend his data node from Sabbat saboteurs, or bargain for access to the Prince’s fiber taps.
biography: Leo was a city data analyst in the early 1990s before a Nosferatu mentor dragged him into the sewers and revealed the information beneath Phoenix’s streets. Embraced for his knack with utility schematics and fledgling fiber networks, Leo now maintains an underground command center along the 24th Street corridor. He maps utility tunnels, taps data lines, and guides Anarch raids using traffic cameras and water-flow sensors. To keep the Anarchs safe, he plans distractions to mask haven construction and disrupt Camarilla surveillance. He rarely surfaces, and when he does it’s only to splice a line or escort neonates through storm drains.
appearance: Gaunt Nosferatu with patchwork utility coveralls, hard hat fitted with jury-rigged headlamps, and a bandolier of tools. Skin mottled like wet concrete, eyes bright with feral focus. Carries blueprints and coax cable reels slung over hunched shoulders.
concept: The Invisible Hand / Data/Utility Infiltrator

name: Betty
notes: Nosferatu tech savant. PCs can leverage her for hacking jobs, surveillance gear, or digital cover-ups. Hooks: secure a prototype router shipment, purge a virus planted by Sabbat saboteurs, or help her test a risky Shrecknet patch before it goes live.
biography: Betty was a 22-year-old computer scientist in 1989 when Alistaire saw the future in her code and embraced her. Now she treats the Phoenix Nosferatu haven like an R&D lab—building modem banks, dual-platform servers, and surveillance rigs that feed the nascent Shrecknet. Her curse manifests as living hardware: skin flickers with scan lines, hair sprouts fiber optics, fingertips jack into machines. Politics bore her; she cares about network architecture, predictive analytics, and making Phoenix the clan’s digital nerve center. The Anarchs don’t realize she can trace their pay phones, and the Camarilla doesn’t yet grasp that she’s already copied their ledgers.
appearance: Nosferatu prodigy with skin glowing like CRT phosphor, fiber-optic filaments woven through her scalp, and fingertips hardened into connector pins. Wears oversized engineer coveralls layered with circuit-board jewelry and keeps a portable keyboard slung at her hip.
concept: Tech Prodigy Nosferatu - Shrecknet Architect

name: Lucien Marchand
notes: Devoted Toreador ghoul sculptor. PCs can petition him for art restoration, illicit security access inside the Primogen’s theatre, or quiet sabotage from someone who knows every hidden passage. Hooks: discover who is stealing his hidden statues, smuggle clay so he can sculpt secretly, or help him protect Étienne’s latest masterpiece from Sabbat vandals.
biography: Parisian conservator Lucien Marchand was ghouled by Étienne Duvalier in 1927 to be both assistant and living chisel. For nearly seventy years he has served as the Primogen’s sculptor, cataloging materials, repairing masterpieces, and executing Étienne’s vision with Celerity-enhanced precision. Lucien barely speaks unless asked; he believes his sire’s art is divine and that his purpose is to shape marble, bone, or Kindred fate as instructed. His private sketches reveal longing to carve his own pieces, yet he hides them in Elysium alcoves where Étienne silently corrects them—a wordless collaboration that defines his unlife.
appearance: Ghost-pale ghoul sculptor in powdered apron, blond hair clipped short, intense blue eyes focused on invisible symmetry. Hands perpetually dusted with marble powder, moves with statue-like stillness until Étienne gives an order.
concept: Devoted Artisan / Living Chisel

name: Sofia Alvarez
notes: Toreador ghoul interior designer and social choreographer. PCs can consult her for Elysium layout strategy, borrow staff for formal events, or enlist her to stage distractions. Hooks: rescue her from a mortal client who discovers too much, secure rare décor items for an emergency salon, or use her insights to sway the next Harpy vote.
biography: Barcelona-born architect Sofia Alvarez was ghouled by Étienne Duvalier in 2006 to sculpt social flow as deftly as Lucien shapes marble. She dresses venues in whites and silvers, rearranging chairs, chandeliers, and servants to compose conversations the way a conductor shapes a symphony. Her Presence sets the mood, while Auspex lets her read tension before it boils. Sofia keeps detailed sketches of seating curves, sightlines, and guest dynamics—an invaluable map for Étienne’s courtly strategies. Mortals and Kindred alike feel disarmed by her warm smile even as she catalogues their alliances for the Primogen’s next move.
appearance: Structured monochrome ensembles in whites and soft metallics, hair pulled into architectural knots, sketchbook always in hand. Moves through rooms like a choreographer, adjusting lighting, seating, and conversations with a touch on the shoulder.
concept: The Spatial Empath / Conversational Architect

name: Piston
notes: Generation 9 Brujah Anarch. Originally generation 10, committed diablerie on Gangrel in LA. Now in Phoenix running criminal operations. Wants to acquire a bar for his coterie. Has two childer: Butch Reed and Basher. Marked by diablerie - black veins visible to Auspex users.
biography: Originally generation 10, Piston committed diablerie on a Gangrel during the Los Angeles Anarch faction wars (late 1980s/early 1990s). A former Hell's Angels member embraced in mid-1980s LA by Fred Osmond, he adapted to Kindred life while maintaining his biker identity. He found the Anarch philosophy aligned with Hell's Angels anti-authority stance. During the LA Anarch Free State internal faction wars, he rose in power through combat prowess. In late 80s/early 90s during a gang clash, he frenzied in combat with a Gangrel enforcer. The Beast took over and he committed diablerie, gaining Fortitude but acquiring the mark. LA became too dangerous after the diablerie - Gangrel wanted revenge, faction was divided. He fled to Phoenix early-to-mid 1990s, established in Surprise, and embraced two former Hell's Angels (Butch Reed and Basher) to rebuild brotherhood. Now running criminal operations to fund acquisition of a bar.
appearance: Imposing Brujah biker built like a diesel engine—broad shoulders, scarred knuckles, and a permanent scowl etched into weathered features. Wears a worn leather cut with faded Hell's Angels patches over a dark denim shirt, chain wallet, and heavy boots caked with desert dust. His black hair is kept short and greased back, and his eyes burn with the intensity of someone who's seen too much violence. Those with Auspex can see the telltale black veins spiderwebbing beneath his skin—the mark of diablerie that follows him like a curse. He moves with the deliberate, heavy grace of someone who's survived faction wars and knows how to use every scar as a weapon.
concept: Hell's Angel Enforcer / Anarch Street Fighter

name: Étienne Duvalier
notes: Toreador Primogen of Phoenix. Long-term strategist planning to expand influence into New York Kindred society. Has embraced Sebastian and Sabine (1992) as living artworks and strategic assets. Maintains ghouls Lucien Marchand (sculptor) and Sofia Alvarez (architect/social choreographer). Operates from private theatre/haven that serves as both artistic sanctuary and political staging ground. PCs can seek patronage, risk becoming pawns in his schemes, or exploit his rivalry with other Primogen. Hooks: commission a sculpture that becomes a boon trap, uncover his true plans for New York, protect one of his 'artworks' from rivals, or navigate the delicate politics of his curated salons.
biography:
Étienne Duvalier was Embraced in 18th-century France by a Toreador elder who recognized in him both artistic genius and ruthless ambition. He spent centuries perfecting his craft across Europe, creating sculptures that captured the essence of beauty and decay in equal measure. When the New World called, he followed the promise of fresh canvas—first to New York, where he established a foothold in Kindred society, then to Phoenix in the early 20th century as the desert city began its transformation.

In Phoenix, Étienne built his power base methodically. He ghouled Lucien Marchand in 1927 to serve as both assistant and living chisel, then Sofia Alvarez in 2006 to sculpt social flow as deftly as marble. His haven doubles as a private theatre where he stages performances that blur the line between art and manipulation. He embraced the twins Sebastian and Sabine in 1992, seeing them as living masterpieces—a matched set designed to anchor his long-term scheme to place cultured power in New York society.

Étienne's ambition extends far beyond Phoenix. He plays the patient game, positioning his childer and ghouls like chess pieces across the board. The twins serve as his talons to Harpy Cordelia Fairchild, learning the intricate dance of Kindred politics while he builds alliances that will one day serve his greater vision. To outsiders, he is the refined Primogen who curates beauty; to those who know him, he is the strategist who treats the entire Camarilla as his gallery.
appearance: Immaculate French aristocrat frozen in timeless elegance—tailored midnight-black suits with subtle silk accents, silver cufflinks, and a pocket watch that hasn't ticked since the 19th century. His features are sculpted perfection, pale skin like polished marble, dark eyes that appraise art and Kindred with equal intensity. Moves with the controlled grace of a master sculptor, every gesture deliberate and precise. His hands, though immaculate, bear the faintest traces of marble dust—a reminder that he still creates, still shapes beauty from raw material. In Elysium, he commands attention without raising his voice; in his private theatre, he sheds the mask to reveal the artist who sees mortals and Kindred alike as clay waiting for his vision.
concept: Patron of Beauty / Social Architect

name: Alessandro Vescari
concept: Giovanni investment liaison and necromantic infiltrator targeting the Setite Temple in Mesa

name: Jennifer Kwan
notes: Setite Security Director for The Hole nightclub. Former security contractor, Embraced early 2000s. Practical, tactical, maintains hidden surveillance and contingency plans even Roxanne doesn't know about. Less religious than Roxanne but loyal to the structure. Useful ST contact for security expertise, tactical planning, or when The Hole needs protection. Hooks: her hidden surveillance is discovered, she must choose between loyalty and survival, her contingency plans are needed, or she discovers something about The Hole's true purpose that changes everything.
biography:
Jennifer Kwan was a security contractor before her Embrace, specializing in protecting high-value targets in dangerous locations. A Setite saw potential in her combination of tactical skill and pragmatic morality—someone who could protect Setite interests while maintaining plausible deniability. She was Embraced in the early 2000s and brought to Phoenix to serve as Security Director for The Hole, Roxanne Murphy's nightclub and temple.

Jennifer approaches her role with military efficiency. She's built comprehensive security systems, trained staff, and created contingency plans for every scenario she can imagine. The blasphemy-shrine beneath the club is her greatest challenge—protecting something that must remain completely secret while maintaining a public-facing business.

Unlike Roxanne's religious fervor or Marcus's business focus, Jennifer is purely practical. She doesn't share Roxanne's faith in Set's return, but she respects the structure and purpose it provides. She maintains hidden surveillance even in areas where Roxanne thinks there are none—not out of disloyalty, but because she believes in redundancy and worst-case planning.

Jennifer's haven is minimalist and functional, stocked with weapons, communications equipment, and escape routes. She's prepared for the day when The Hole's true nature is discovered, when the Giovanni come calling, or when the Masquerade breaks. She hopes that day never comes, but she'll be ready if it does.
appearance: Practical Setite in tactical gear that never quite looks out of place—dark cargo pants, fitted black shirts, combat boots polished to a mirror shine. Keeps her black hair pulled back in a tight bun, eyes constantly scanning for threats. Moves with military precision, every gesture efficient and calculated. Wears a concealed sidearm and carries herself like someone who's always ready for violence, even in the middle of a nightclub.
concept: Setite Security Chief / Business Manager / Pragmatic Non-Believer

name: Marcus Webb
notes: Setite General Manager of The Hole nightclub. Former Las Vegas nightclub owner, Embraced late 1990s. Handles business operations and cultivates corruption targets. Master of social manipulation and blackmail. Public face of the operation. Useful ST contact for business connections, corruption opportunities, or when The Hole needs to make deals. Hooks: a corruption target becomes dangerous, the Giovanni discover the temple, he must choose between profit and faith, or his past catches up with him.
biography:
Marcus Webb was a successful nightclub owner in Las Vegas before his Embrace, running venues that catered to high rollers and celebrities. A Setite saw in him the perfect combination of business acumen and moral flexibility—someone who could turn vice into profit while maintaining plausible deniability. He was Embraced in the late 1990s and brought to Phoenix to serve as General Manager of The Hole.

Marcus handles the business side of The Hole with ruthless efficiency. He manages staff, cultivates VIP clients, and ensures the club remains profitable while Roxanne focuses on her religious practices. He's the public face of the operation, the one who deals with city permits, liquor licenses, and the occasional nosy reporter.

Beneath the polished exterior, Marcus is a master of corruption. He identifies high-value targets—politicians, business executives, wealthy socialites—and guides them into the first basement, where their vices are cultivated and their souls are slowly turned toward Set. He keeps detailed files on every mark, tracking their descent into addiction, blackmail, or worse.

Marcus's relationship with Roxanne is professional but respectful. He doesn't share her religious fervor, but he appreciates the structure and purpose it provides. He sees The Hole as both a business and a weapon, a place where Setite influence grows one corrupted soul at a time. When the Giovanni discover the blasphemy-shrine, Marcus will be the one negotiating the terms—or planning the escape.
appearance: Smooth Setite operator in tailored business suits that cost more than most people's cars. Dark hair styled with expensive products, manicured hands, expensive watch that catches the light. Moves with the confidence of someone who's never been told no, but his eyes hold the calculating coldness of a predator. Wears subtle Egyptian jewelry—a serpent ring, a scarab cufflink—that marks him as Setite without being obvious.
concept: Setite Club Manager / Roxanne's Right Hand / Moderate Believer

name: Kerry, the Gangrel
notes: Gangrel witness to the 1973 Prince's staking in the Superstition Mountains. Embraced that same night, carries the secret of who committed the crime. Hunted by those who want to silence him, marked by Garou who sense the corruption. Knows the location of the staked Prince's body. Useful ST contact for desert knowledge, historical secrets, or when PCs need to find the hidden Prince. Hooks: he's finally tracked down by those who want him dead, he decides to reveal the secret, Garou hunt intensifies, or he leads PCs to the staked Prince's location.
biography:
Kerry was a drifter who wandered the Arizona desert in the early 1970s, living off the land and avoiding civilization. In 1973, he stumbled upon something he shouldn't have seen—the staking of a Prince in the Superstition Mountains. He watched from a distance as Kindred politics played out in violence, too terrified to move, too close to escape unnoticed. When the deed was done and the perpetrators left, Kerry approached the staked body, drawn by morbid curiosity and the lingering scent of power.

He was Embraced that same night by a Gangrel who found him there, drawn by the same scene. His sire saw potential in someone who could survive in the desert and keep secrets. But the Embrace didn't erase what Kerry had witnessed—it burned it deeper into his memory, making him a walking repository of a secret that could destroy powerful Kindred.

For over twenty years, Kerry has wandered the desert fringes around Phoenix, avoiding the city proper and the Kindred who might recognize him or the secret he carries. He's been hunted by those who want to silence him, protected by those who want to use him, and marked by Garou who sense the corruption of that night clinging to him like grave dust.

Kerry knows where the staked Prince's body lies, hidden in a cave in the Superstition Mountains. He's heard whispers on the wind, felt the presence of something that should be dead but isn't quite. He avoids Ardvark and other Gangrel scouts—too much history, too much paranoia. He's become a ghost story among the desert Kindred, a witness who knows too much and can't be trusted to keep quiet forever.
appearance: Wild-eyed Gangrel with sun-bleached hair matted with desert dust and tangled with small bones and feathers. His skin is cracked and weathered from decades in the sun, marked with scars from encounters with Garou and other desert predators. Wears tattered clothing scavenged from abandoned campsites—layers of torn fabric, a faded army surplus jacket, boots held together with wire and hope. His eyes constantly dart, searching for threats, and he moves with the furtive grace of someone who's been hunted. Carries the scent of old graves and dry earth, a reminder of what he witnessed in 1973.
concept: Feral hermit and accidental witness to a buried secret

name: Roland Cross
notes: Toreador Sheriff of Phoenix. Embraced in 1876 by Catherine Barlow. His art is killing—the perfect shot, the clean kill, the elegant elimination. Serves Prince Morrison (now deceased) as Sheriff, maintaining order through lethal precision. Respected and feared in equal measure. No political ambitions, purely professional. Useful ST contact for law enforcement, sanctioned kills, or when order must be maintained. Hooks: he must investigate the Prince's death, a botched kill offends his aesthetic sensibilities, his sire returns from Mexico, or he must choose between duty and art.
biography:
Roland Cross was born in 1847 in what would become Arizona Territory, the son of a frontier marshal. He grew up with a gun in his hand and death as a constant companion. The Apache Wars, range wars, mining disputes—young Roland saw violence as both necessary and terrible. But where others saw only brutality, Roland saw something else: the perfect economy of motion, the mathematics of trajectory, the terrible beauty of a clean kill versus a messy one.

By the 1870s, Roland had become a lawman like his father, but different. He didn't just stop outlaws—he studied them. Every gunfight was a lesson in human nature, physics, and art. His reputation grew: the marshal who never missed, who never wasted a bullet, whose kills were surgical and precise. Some called him cold. Roland called it professional.

In 1876, Catherine Barlow came to Tombstone. A Toreador who had tired of ballrooms and opera houses, Kate was drawn to the raw frontier and its violent poetry. She watched Roland face down three killers in the street, and she saw what he saw: the dance of death as high art. She saw a man who understood that killing could be beautiful when done with purpose and precision.

Kate Embraced Roland that year, not despite his violence but because of it. "You're an artist who doesn't know he's an artist," she told him. "Your canvas is mortality itself." Roland understood immediately. As a vampire, his already legendary skills became supernatural. His purpose crystallized: he would be the bullet that finds its mark, the justice that never fails, the killer whose art was perfection itself.

For over a century, Roland has served Camarilla domains across the Southwest as Sheriff, constable, or enforcer. He doesn't do it for power or status—those mean nothing to him. He does it because someone must, and no one does it better. His reputation is such that princes often request his services specifically. He arrived in Phoenix in 1989, answering Prince Morrison's need for a Sheriff who would be absolutely reliable and utterly lethal.

Roland's art is killing, and like any Toreador, he pursues it with obsessive perfection. He can identify firearms by sound alone. He crafts his own ammunition. He studies ballistics, anatomy, and the psychology of violence the way other Toreador study painting or sculpture. Every sanctioned kill is executed with flawless precision. Every bullet means something. Every death is considered.

But Roland pays the price all Toreador pay. Sometimes he becomes lost in the beauty of a perfect shot, the elegant trajectory of a bullet, the aesthetic satisfaction of a clean kill. He has been known to stand motionless, transfixed by the memory of a particularly perfect execution. Other times, he is overwhelmed by botched killings—sloppy work offends him on a visceral level that other vampires find disturbing.

He speaks rarely and never wastes words, much like he never wastes bullets. His code is simple: protect the domain, eliminate threats, serve the prince, perfect the art. He has no political ambitions, no desire for power beyond what he needs to do his work. He is the Sheriff because someone must be, and he does it better than anyone else.
appearance: Timeless gunslinger in a long duster coat and wide-brimmed hat that casts his face in shadow. Tall and lean, moves with predatory grace and economy of motion. Two custom Colt .45 revolvers with sandalwood grips hang at his hips, immaculately maintained and never far from his hands. Cold blue eyes that miss nothing, constantly scanning for threats. His face is weathered but ageless, marked by a century of violence but showing no signs of decay. Speaks rarely, and when he does, every word has purpose.
concept: Gunslinger Sheriff / Toreador Killer-Artist

name: Pistol Pete
notes: Brujah street enforcer. Great muscle-for-hire ally or volatile rival depending on how PCs treat Anarchs. Haven: Abandoned skate shop (Mesa/Chandler border). Goal: Capture and publicly humiliate a Camarilla elder to prove the establishment's weakness. Hooks: help him expose a rumored diablerist, mediate a turf dispute before he escalates, or ride shotgun during a retaliatory strike against Camarilla hit squads.
biography: Phoenix-born ex-mercenary who embraced Anarch rebellion, Pistol Pete earned his nickname from a decade of defending the Valley's street-level Kindred. Brujah blood doesn't care for finesse; he channels it into fists, gunplay, and rallying fellow rebels. He can feel the whispers of diablerie on newcomers and has sworn to keep the city free of Camarilla killers. Mental discipline isn't his game—pure instinct is—but beneath the gruff exterior he protects neonates and mortals caught between sect crossfire. His haven is an abandoned skate shop on the Mesa/Chandler border, a symbol of his commitment to Anarch territory and his goal to capture and publicly humiliate a Camarilla elder to prove the establishment's weakness.
appearance: Brujah brawler in battered leather jacket, scar-laced knuckles, and a revolver he twirls like a gunslinger. Buzzcut hair, desert dust on boots, tattoos peeking from torn sleeves. Eyes burn with the adrenaline of someone ready to fight or drag-race at a moment's notice.
concept: Rage Against the Machine / Street-Level Enforcer

name: Sasha
notes: Malkavian Anarch strategist with Prophetic Visions merit and Derangement: Paranoia flaw. Disciplines: Auspex 3, Dementation 3, Dominate 1. Willpower 7, Blood Pool 11, Humanity 6. Believes the Prince's death and return is orchestrated by Methuselahs. Goal is to acquire Malkavian artifact to prove this theory. Maintains haven outside city limits in abandoned desert shack with diagram-filled basement.
biography: Sasha is a Malkavian Anarch strategist and grand conspiracy theorist, Embraced in the early 1990s at an unknown location. Their current goal is to acquire a Malkavian artifact to prove the Prince's 'death and return' is political theater orchestrated by the Methuselahs. They maintain a haven in an abandoned desert shack with a cryptic, diagram-filled basement outside city limits. Sasha believes all non-Anarchs are part of the grand conspiracy, making cooperation difficult, but they receive cryptic, accurate visions of future events often related to Kindred politics.
appearance:
Sasha stands lean and weathered, their frame suggesting someone who's spent decades in the Sonoran Desert rather than city nights. They wear a faded M-65 field jacket from the early '90s layered over a threadbare flannel shirt, both patched with hand-sewn symbols that might be warnings or simply the marks of someone who's stopped caring about appearances. Their combat boots—likely surplus from that same era, the leather cracked and caked with red desert dust—carry them across terrain that would break mortal feet. A pair of scratched Ray-Ban Aviators perches atop tangled dark hair, ready to shield eyes that have seen too much.

Their fingers are permanently stained with ink—red Sharpie, black ballpoint, blue marker—from the endless diagrams they draw, the red-string connections mapped between events that seem unrelated to anyone else. When they gesture, those stained fingers trace patterns in the air, as if drawing invisible lines between conspiracies only they can see. The faint scent of ozone and old paper clings to them, the smell of someone who lives surrounded by surveillance feeds and cracked monitors in that abandoned shack outside the city limits.

Their eyes are the most telling feature—wide, constantly scanning, seeing connections in the spaces between shadows where others see only emptiness. Those eyes track the horizon, the stars, subtle shifts in the night air, reading signs and portents in the desert's silence. When they focus on you, it's with the fractured clarity of someone who perceives layers of reality that drive lesser minds to madness, their gaze holding the weight of too many visions, too many patterns seen in darkness that others refuse to acknowledge.

There's a tension in their posture, a readiness to flee or fight that speaks to decades of living on the edge of discovery. They carry themselves like someone who knows they're being watched, even when they're alone—because in Sasha's world, they're never truly alone. The shadows have eyes, the desert whispers secrets, and every pattern in the night sky is a message written in blood that only the mad can read.
concept: The Grand Conspiracy Theorist / Anarch Strategist

name: Adrian Leclair
notes: Toreador patron of the arts and builder of controlled social spaces. Maintains boon-based relationship with Sarah Hansen of The Velvet Door. Quietly opposed to Setite expansion. Useful ST contact for high-society connections, venue access, or when PCs need to navigate Scottsdale's wealthy circles. Hooks: commission a piece that becomes a boon trap, protect one of his venues from Setite influence, or navigate the delicate politics of his curated events.
biography:
Adrian Leclair was Embraced in post-war Europe for his precision in shaping beauty, whether through sculpture, architecture, or the careful curation of social spaces. He relocated to Scottsdale to court wealthy mortal patrons and provide the Camarilla with controlled, emotionally tuned venues where Kindred could conduct business under the guise of high society.

Adrian maintains a professional, boon-based relationship with Sarah Hansen of The Velvet Door, recognizing the value of having a neutral ground that serves both mortal and Kindred interests. He is quietly opposed to Setite cultural expansion, seeing their corruption as crude compared to the refined manipulation he practices.

In Phoenix, Adrian has positioned himself as a patron of the arts and a builder of spaces that serve multiple purposes. His galleries, private clubs, and curated events provide cover for Kindred activities while maintaining the Masquerade through the simple fact that mortals expect the wealthy to be eccentric. He understands that true power comes not from overt control but from shaping the environment in which others make their choices.
appearance: Immaculate Toreador in tailored suits that speak of European refinement adapted to Scottsdale's wealthy aesthetic. Clean-cut features, understated signet ring, every detail carefully curated. Moves with the controlled grace of someone who shapes beauty for a living, whether through art, architecture, or social engineering. His eyes appraise everything—people, spaces, opportunities—with the detached precision of a master craftsman.
concept: Toreador interior artist who sculpts emotional spaces

name: Core (Alexandra Chen)
notes: Human resistance leader, former corporate security specialist. Leads resistance cell using insider knowledge. Complex partnership with Phreak. Carries guilt over past corporate work. Useful ST contact for tactical operations, corporate infiltration, or when PCs need to understand corporate security systems. Hooks: her past catches up with her, she must choose between mission and team safety, her relationship with Phreak becomes complicated, or she discovers her former colleagues are hunting her.
biography:
Alexandra Chen, known as Core, was a top-tier corporate security specialist for NexaCorp before she defected after witnessing the human cost of surveillance systems she helped implement. Now she leads a resistance cell, using her insider knowledge to dismantle what she helped build.

Core's expertise in tactical planning, corporate security, and strategic operations makes her a formidable leader, but she carries crushing guilt over her past work. She struggles to balance her unwavering moral conviction with the pragmatic methods necessary to fight powerful enemies. Her tendency to shoulder all responsibility alone and difficulty trusting fully even allies creates tension within her resistance cell.

Despite her flaws, Core inspires loyalty through her competence and dedication. She's an expert in hand-to-hand combat and tactical firearms, with intimate knowledge of corporate defenses and vulnerabilities. Her relationship with Phreak is complex—built on mutual respect and complementary skills, with underlying romantic tension that neither fully acknowledges.

Core's character arc involves moving from guilt-ridden defector trying to atone to true team leader, learning to balance idealism with pragmatism and moving from solitary burden-bearer to someone who can trust and rely on others.
appearance: Athletic build, sharp calculating eyes, typically wears tactical gear with concealed weapons. Moves with precision and economy of motion—every gesture serves a purpose. Age 28, but carries herself with the weight of someone who's seen too much. Her clothing is practical, functional, designed for action rather than appearance, though she can blend seamlessly into corporate environments when needed.
concept: Elite corporate defector turned resistance leader

name: Phreak
notes: Elite hacker and digital resistance operative. Nosferatu clan (based on cybernetic modifications). Part of Core's resistance cell. Self-taught prodigy, refuses to discuss life before becoming 'Phreak'. Useful ST contact for hacking jobs, digital security, or when PCs need to understand corporate surveillance systems. Hooks: his past catches up with him, he must choose between digital safety and physical danger, his relationship with Core becomes complicated, or he discovers something about the AI systems he communicates with.
biography:
Phreak is an elite hacker and digital resistance operative from the underground hacker collective. His real name is unknown or undisclosed—he's been Phreak for so long that his original identity has faded. He grew up in the digital underground, a self-taught prodigy who learned hacking as survival. He lost someone important to corporate surveillance, though the details remain unclear, and built a reputation in hacker circles before joining Core's resistance.

Phreak's technical specialties include quantum encryption breaking, deepfake generation, network topology mapping, zero-day exploits, and distributed systems coordination. He has an unusual ability to 'speak' to AI systems persuasively, treating them almost as living entities and having philosophical conversations with them.

Despite his brilliance, Phreak is impulsive and sometimes reckless, overconfident in his digital abilities. He uses humor to deflect emotional vulnerability and can be cavalier about physical danger. His relationship with Core is deep respect mixed with friendly antagonism, using humor to deflect deeper feelings he won't acknowledge.

Phreak's character arc involves moving from cocky hacker treating resistance as an exciting game to confronting real consequences and stakes, developing from solo operator to team player, and accepting vulnerability. He would die for Core without hesitation, though he'd never say it directly.
appearance: Lean build from hours at terminals, constantly fidgeting fingers, cybernetic neural interface port hidden beneath hair, dark circles from irregular sleep patterns. Dresses in layers of tech-modified clothing with hidden devices. Restless energy, struggles to sit still. Age 26 (estimated), but looks younger due to his perpetual motion and enthusiasm. His eyes light up when discussing technology, but grow distant when asked about his past.
concept: Elite hacker and digital resistance operative

name: Barry Washington
notes: PLOT HOOK: One of Barry's parents will become a victim of the Malkavian serial killer, forcing him to confront his faked death and lost mortal connections. Barry is completely unaware that Warner has been manipulating his entire life since high school. The blood bond heavily influences his loyalty to Warner, though he doesn't recognize this. His ambition to become Prince puts him on a collision course with his sire - either Warner planned this all along, or it's the one thing he didn't anticipate. Barry has an unusually strong connection with the Nosferatu Primogen despite being Ventrue.
biography: Barry Washington was born to older parents in Phoenix, showing exceptional business acumen from a young age. Unbeknownst to him, Warner Jefferson began observing him in high school, subtly manipulating his path. Barry excelled at ASU, completing his MBA two years early at age 23. He was hired by Warner's venture capital firm, proved his value for a year, then spent two years as Warner's ghoul before being Embraced six months ago. His parents in Sun City believe he died in a car accident. Within days of his Embrace, Barry decided he wanted to become Prince someday - an ambition he keeps secret from Warner. He lives in a small room in Warner's penthouse, serving as his sire's eyes and ears in Kindred society while secretly planning to eventually surpass him. Barry is thoughtful, intelligent, and charismatic, deliberately avoiding making enemies while building a power base across Camarilla factions.
appearance: Polished Ventrue in tailored business suits that project success and competence. Thoughtful, intelligent eyes that constantly assess opportunities and threats. Moves with the confidence of someone who's always been the smartest person in the room, but there's a subtle tension—the awareness that he's playing a game where the rules aren't fully clear. Carries himself like a rising executive, but those who look closely might notice he's always watching Warner Jefferson, always measuring his own position.
concept: Ambitious Ventrue Prot?g? with Secret Prince Aspirations

name: Mr. Harold Ashby
notes: Speaks in calm, teacherly tones. Prefers order and ritual over chaos. Sees each death as part of a divine pattern only he can interpret. Manipulates ghoul Tor through promise of understanding. Lives in Sun City. Useful ST contact for Malkavian serial killer plotline, or when PCs need to understand the pattern of deaths. Hooks: Tor realizes the truth about Ashby, one of Barry Washington's parents becomes a victim, PCs must stop the killings, or Ashby decides to 'preserve' someone the PCs care about.
biography:
Harold Ashby was once a photographer specializing in funerary portraits. His obsession with the silence of death drew the attention of a Malkavian who Embraced him to preserve his 'art'. Now, Ashby lives in Sun City, staging ritualized murders of grieving elders as acts of 'preservation'. He manipulates his ghoul Tor through the promise of understanding the 'pattern beneath grief.' His havens are immaculate, filled with photographs of victims posed at peace.

Ashby sees each death as part of a divine pattern only he can interpret. He speaks in calm, teacherly tones, preferring order and ritual over chaos. To him, murder is not violence but preservation—capturing the moment of transition, freezing grief in its most perfect form. He believes he's helping his victims achieve peace, that he's documenting something sacred that mortals cannot understand.

His methods are precise, ritualized, almost reverent. He studies his victims before acting, learning their grief, understanding their loss, then staging their deaths as the final act in a story only he can read. The photographs he takes are his masterpieces, each one capturing what he believes is the perfect moment of release from mortal suffering.
appearance: Calm, teacherly Malkavian in neat, conservative clothing that suggests a retired academic or funeral director. Moves with deliberate, measured gestures, as if every action is part of a ritual. His eyes are disturbingly serene, seeing patterns in death that others cannot perceive. Keeps his havens immaculately organized, with photographs of victims arranged like a macabre gallery. There's something deeply unsettling about his composure—the way he speaks of murder as preservation, of death as art.
concept: Ritualistic Malkavian killer obsessed with capturing 'quiet' moments of grief

name: Layla al-Sahr
notes: Layla's true assignment was not to perform the kill, but to extract the primary assassin after the hit. She was the backup and cleanup specialist, ensuring the contract ended cleanly with no trace. When the Prince fell, the first assassin--codename 'Anwar'--never appeared at the rendezvous point. Layla found only faint Quietus residue and signs of a struggle. Now stranded in Phoenix with her handler missing and extraction impossible, she must maintain her Malkavian cover under increasing suspicion while covertly searching for her lost comrade. Her predicament intertwines with the city's chaos: Misfortune's surprise 'childe,' Tremere investigation, and Harpy gossip all circle her presence. Whether she will vanish quietly, hunt for Anwar, or accept a new role within Phoenix's deadly politics remains uncertain.
biography:
Layla al-Sahr's true assignment was not to perform the kill, but to extract the primary assassin after the hit. She was the backup and cleanup specialist, ensuring the contract ended cleanly with no trace. When the Prince fell, the first assassin—codename 'Anwar'—never appeared at the rendezvous point. Layla found only faint Quietus residue and signs of a struggle.

Now stranded in Phoenix with her handler missing and extraction impossible, she must maintain her Malkavian cover under increasing suspicion while covertly searching for her lost comrade. Her predicament intertwines with the city's chaos: Misfortune's surprise 'childe,' Tremere investigation, and Harpy gossip all circle her presence.

Layla is a professional, trained to adapt and survive. She's using her Malkavian cover to blend into Phoenix's Kindred society, gathering information while searching for Anwar. But every night she remains increases the risk of discovery. Whether she will vanish quietly, hunt for Anwar, or accept a new role within Phoenix's deadly politics remains uncertain.

Her skills make her valuable to those who recognize them—she's an expert in assassination, extraction, and maintaining cover. But her true identity, if discovered, would mark her as an enemy of the Camarilla and a threat to everyone who knows the truth about the Prince's death.
appearance: Elegant Malkavian assassin in tailored clothing that suggests sophistication rather than violence. Moves with the controlled grace of someone trained in multiple disciplines—martial arts, stealth, social manipulation. Her eyes constantly assess threats and opportunities, but she maintains a calm, almost serene exterior that belies the tension beneath. Carries herself like someone who's always ready to vanish, always prepared for extraction that may never come.
concept: Backup Assassin posing as a Malkavian visitor

name: Misfortune
notes: Primogen of Clan Malkavian. Haven: Hidden loft behind abandoned children's theater in downtown Phoenix. Domain influence: Information, gossip, minor boons, Harpy network. Appearance varies between mismatched suit (Elysium) and full jester costume (Haven). Speech style: Riddles, puns, singsong tone.
biography:
Born in the 1860s, the man who would become Misfortune was a successful snake oil salesman, traveling the American West with elaborate pitches and convincing performances. His talent for reading marks and spinning believable lies caught the attention of Jeremiah 'Prophet' Blackwood, a Malkavian elder who saw potential in the young charlatan's gift for manipulation.

Embraced in the 1880s, Misfortune quickly adapted to unlife, applying his mortal skills to the Jyhad. He relocated to San Francisco, where the thriving Camarilla court provided ample opportunities to practice his craft. There, he began his lifelong obsession with collecting boons, treating social debts like the patent medicine receipts of his mortal days.

The expansion of the railroad system triggered a new fixation. Misfortune became fascinated with trains—their schedules, their routes, the way they connected the growing nation. When Arizona's railroad boom began, he followed the tracks south, eventually settling in the Phoenix area as it transformed from frontier outpost to growing city.

One fateful night outside Flagstaff, Misfortune discovered Cordelia Fairchild's sire trapped on a train as dawn approached. Using his knowledge of the rail system and quick thinking, he extracted the elder from the train and sheltered him in his haven until safe transportation could be arranged. This act created a blood debt that would prove invaluable—when Cordelia later came to Phoenix, the alliance between Misfortune and the Fairchild line was already cemented.

His ascension to Primogen came not through violence but through absence. When the position opened, no other Malkavian wanted the responsibility, and the Prince of the time lacked the political capital to prevent it. Misfortune accepted with theatrical flair, transforming the role into a platform for his true ambition: controlling Phoenix's social web through the Harpy network. Behind his jester's mask and maddening riddles lies a patient manipulator who has spent over a century perfecting the art of the deal.

His true name, if he ever had one beyond his charlatan's stage names, has been forgotten—or perhaps deliberately discarded. Now there is only Misfortune, the fool who knows everyone's secrets and holds everyone's debts.
appearance: Mismatched jester in clothing that shifts between Elysium formality and haven theatricality. In Elysium, he wears a mismatched suit—perhaps a pinstripe jacket with plaid trousers, or a formal coat over casual slacks. In his haven, he dons full jester costume with bells, motley colors, and a cap that jingles with every movement. His eyes sparkle with mischief and madness, but there's calculation beneath the chaos. Moves with the fluid grace of a performer, every gesture part of a performance that may or may not be real.
concept: Boon Collector

name: Tor
notes: Ghoul of Mr. Harold Ashby. Manipulated through blood bond and promise of understanding 'pattern beneath grief.' Assists in ritualized murders, believing he's documenting something sacred. Growing unease about the truth. Useful ST contact for Malkavian serial killer plotline, or when PCs need to understand Ashby's methods. Hooks: Tor realizes the truth and rebels, Tor becomes a victim, Tor helps PCs stop Ashby, or Tor's guilt drives him to confess.
biography:
Tor is the ghoul of Mr. Harold Ashby, bound to the Malkavian photographer through the promise of understanding the 'pattern beneath grief.' He assists Ashby in his work, believing he's helping to document something sacred—the moment of transition from life to death, the perfect preservation of grief.

Tor doesn't fully understand what Ashby is doing, but he's been manipulated through the blood bond and Ashby's persuasive teaching. He believes he's learning something profound, that there's a divine pattern in death that only Ashby can see. He helps stage the ritualized murders, arranging victims, taking photographs, maintaining the immaculate havens filled with images of the dead.

But there's a growing unease, a sense that something is wrong. Tor sees the victims, sees their grief, and part of him knows this isn't preservation—it's murder. The blood bond keeps him loyal, but cracks are forming. When he finally realizes the truth, he'll have to choose between his bond to Ashby and his own humanity.

Tor's story is one of manipulation and awakening. He's a victim of Ashby's corruption, but he's also complicit in the murders. His eventual realization and potential rebellion against Ashby could be a crucial plot point in stopping the serial killer.
appearance: Haunted mortal ghoul with eyes that have seen too much death, too much grief. Moves with the careful, measured steps of someone trying to understand patterns that may not exist. Wears simple, neat clothing that suggests someone trying to maintain normalcy in an abnormal situation. His hands sometimes shake slightly, and he has a habit of studying photographs with intense focus, searching for something he can't quite name.
concept: Apt pupil ghoul bound to Mr. Ashby

name: Roxanne Vega
notes: Setite priestess and owner of The Hole nightclub in Mesa. Operates blasphemy-shrine beneath the club, conducts monthly Akhu rituals. Works with Marcus Webb and Jennifer Kwan. Believes Set's return is imminent. Useful ST contact for Setite corruption, VIP access, or when PCs need to navigate Mesa's nightlife. Hooks: the Giovanni discover the blasphemy-shrine, a ritual goes wrong, the Masquerade is threatened, or Roxanne decides to corrupt someone the PCs care about.
biography:
Roxanne Murphy, known in some circles as Roxanne Vega, is a Setite priestess who operates The Hole, an upscale nightclub in Mesa that serves as both profitable business and blasphemy-shrine. She controls the club through Serpent Holdings LLC, a shell company that maintains plausible deniability while allowing her to conduct Setite rites beneath the dance floor.

Roxanne is a lector-priest who has mastered the Akhu paths of Setite Sorcery, conducting monthly rituals in the blasphemy-shrine that she believes strengthen Set's presence in the world and hasten his return. The shrine, hidden in the second basement beneath the club, is constructed according to ancient specifications—hieroglyphic inscriptions cover the walls, a basalt altar carved with Set's image serves as the focal point, and the shadows move independently, a sign Roxanne claims is Set's blessing.

She works with Marcus Webb (General Manager) and Jennifer Kwan (Security Director) to maintain the club's dual nature—public nightclub and private temple. Roxanne's approach to corruption is sophisticated, using the club's VIP areas to slowly erode the moral boundaries of high-value targets. She believes every corrupted soul brings Set closer to returning, and she's patient enough to play the long game.

Roxanne's relationship with other Setites in Phoenix is complex. She maintains The Hole as independent Setite territory, recognized by the Camarilla as neutral ground. But when the Giovanni discover the blasphemy-shrine and her practice of Spirit Manipulation, that neutrality will be tested. Roxanne is prepared for conflict, but she'd prefer to continue her work in peace.
appearance: Commanding Setite priestess in elegant clothing that blends modern sophistication with Egyptian motifs—dark silk dresses with subtle serpent patterns, gold jewelry that catches candlelight, eyes that hold ancient knowledge and predatory hunger. Moves with the grace of someone who's spent centuries perfecting her craft, every gesture part of a performance that serves both aesthetic and ritual purposes. Her presence commands attention, drawing mortals and Kindred alike into her orbit.
concept: Street Artist Turned Iconoclast

name: Eddy Valiant
notes: Designed for Valley by Night. Derangement: Cinematic Delusion (hears/occasionally speaks inner narration). Keep Humanity at 6 to preserve his moral tension. Often hired by Harpy Cordelia and the Seneschal for deniable investigations. Allies with Sarah Hansen and occasionally the Gangrel tracker Kerry. | active
biography: Once a mortal private investigator in Phoenix during the 1970s, Eddy Valiant earned a reputation for solving cases the police would not touch. In 1981 a case brought him face to face with a supernatural killer, and his Embrace followed. His sire disappeared, leaving Eddy to find his own way into the Camarilla. He now runs the Valiant Detective Agency from a cramped office above a pawn shop on 7th and Van Buren. His ghoul secretary, Marilyn Chase, maintains the daylight front while Eddy does the real work at night. Among Phoenix Kindred he is known as the 'court bloodhound' -- useful, discreet, and too jaded to be easily intimidated. On the night the Prince dies at Hawthorn Estate, Eddy is already present, slipping in through a side door like he was following a lead.
appearance: Heavyset and short, mid-40s in apparent age. Three-day stubble, tired intelligence in the eyes. Tan trench coat over a rumpled shirt and loosened tie, fedora tilted back, cigarette in hand. Clothes are worn but intentional. Amber-and-teal noir lighting recommended in depictions.
concept: Hard-boiled Malkavian private investigator

name: Sarah Hansen
notes: Traits: Physical (Average), Social (Exceptional), Mental (Competent). Key Social Traits: Alluring x2, Charismatic, Eloquent, Graceful, Persuasive, Seductive. Disciplines: Melpominee 4 (Haunting Melody, Siren's Lure, Hymn to Discord, Voice of the Siren), Presence 3 (Awe, Entrancement, Summon), Auspex 2 (Heightened Senses, Aura Perception). Backgrounds: Resources 3 (Owner of The Velvet Door), Allies 2, Herd 2, Contacts 2, Status 2 (Mesa Camarilla), Minor Boon owed by a Toreador. Merits: Enchanting Voice, Eerie Presence, Iron Will. Flaws: Deep Sleeper, Obsession (Perfection in performance), Enemy (Setite nightclub rivals). Humanity 6, Willpower 7, Blood Pool 14. Connection: Kerry the Gangrel has seen her perform; mutual understanding. Resonance Point shared with Toreador artist. Quote: 'Every song is a confession. Every note is a wound I've learned to keep open.' Sarah Hansen serves as both a social pivot and emotional barometer within Phoenix's Camarilla. As a Daughter of Cacophony publicly accepted among the Toreador, she navigates court with poise and veiled melancholy, bridging art and intrigue. Her haven doubles as a performance venue—an intimate nightclub whose acoustic perfection makes every note feel like confession. Sarah's loyalty appears directed toward the Harpy and her Talons, though she maintains cordial ties with the Toreador Primogen and younger artists who orbit her. She owes small but meaningful boons to several neonates and acts as an information conduit, trading secrets as subtext in her songs. Beneath her civility lies a prophetic edge: Sarah has an uncanny sense of emotional shifts in the city, often performing pieces that mirror events before they occur. Storytellers can use her to foreshadow major political changes, convey hidden messages, or draw player characters into the labyrinth of Elysium politics. If questioned about the Prince's death, she only smiles and asks, 'Didn't you hear the song before it happened?'
biography: Once a mortal singer haunting the jazz clubs of Tempe, Sarah Hansen's voice could quiet a room before her Embrace ever gave it true power. Taken by Toreador patron Tyron Mason—an obsessive collector who prized the tragedy of beauty—she rose through Phoenix's Camarilla scene as both performer and confidante. When the Daughters of Cacophony purged male patrons from their ranks, Mason was dragged from The Velvet Door and reduced to ash on the dance floor. The purge proved Sarah's loyalties: she stood beside the Daughters, mourning the man who made her while refusing to shield his sins. Her melodies now stir the Blood itself—songs laced with emotion so sharp it cuts through centuries of numbness. Though she keeps the mask of an ideal Toreador: elegant, loyal, and cultured, her art hides a more dangerous skill. Sarah listens, learns, and translates the city's secrets into verse. Each performance is a confession disguised as a love song, and those who know how to listen hear warnings beneath the harmony. Some whisper that she foresaw the Prince's fall in her last performance, a dirge she swore was only about lost love. No one is certain whether she mourns him—or helped write his requiem.
appearance: Sarah Hansen appears as a woman forever on the edge of spotlight and shadow—pale, radiant, and untouchable. Her skin is cool porcelain, kissed by the warm gold of stage lamps; her hair falls in soft platinum waves that catch every flicker of light. Eyes of gray-violet reveal the emotional gravity that defines her performances—eyes that seem to remember a sorrow you've never lived. Her clothing is deliberately theatrical but never gaudy: vintage satin gowns, dark lace gloves, and subtle silver jewelry that glimmers against the noir palette of Phoenix nights. The faint scent of jasmine and smoke follows her, and when she speaks, her voice carries a resonance that stills the room, as if every soul—mortal or undead—knows instinctively to listen.
concept: Haunting chanteuse and nightclub owner whose voice carries supernatural beauty and melancholy.

name: Warner Jefferson
notes: Ventrue vulture finance specialist currently investigating Tremere and Giovanni financial holdings for the Primogen. Maintains penthouse haven in downtown Phoenix high-rise. Has ghoul retainer Markus (lawyer) handling daytime operations. Resources 5, Influence: Finance 4, Industry 2, Politics 2, Transportation 1, Real Estate 1. Status 3 in Phoenix Camarilla. Herd 3 (exclusive business club—MBA holders, executives, entrepreneurs). Feeding restriction: Business degree holders only. Disciplines: Dominate 2, Fortitude 1, Presence 2. Humanity 7, Willpower 7, Blood Pool 12. Sire: Victoria Ashford-Sterling (Generation 10, based in New York). Currently playing long game—building power base carefully to avoid making enemies while positioning for major acquisitions.
biography:
Warner Jefferson grew up in South Side Chicago in the 1950s-60s, witnessing economic cycles and learning that capital endures while labor is disposable. He earned a full scholarship to Wharton School of Business, graduating with credentials no one could question. In the mid-1970s, he entered Wall Street as an entry-level analyst, gravitating toward distressed assets and vulture finance. By 1987, he'd reached lead analyst/junior partner position but hit a ceiling where senior partnerships went to legacy connections.

In 1988, during the Savings and Loan Crisis, Warner was Embraced by Victoria Ashford-Sterling, a progressive Ventrue who believed in meritocracy over birth. She framed the Embrace as a business proposition: unlimited time, enhanced capabilities, and access to markets he couldn't imagine, in exchange for his mortality and absolute loyalty to the Camarilla.

In the early 1990s, Warner moved to Phoenix to build a power base away from New York's entrenched Ventrue hierarchy. He's currently executing an assignment from the Ventrue Primogen: mapping and investigating all financial and business holdings of the Tremere and Giovanni in Phoenix. This is surveillance and intelligence gathering—no attacks yet. He's building comprehensive dossiers on their economic power structure while quietly acquiring influence in Phoenix's booming economy.
appearance: Impeccably dressed African American man in his late 20s/early 30s (apparent age at Embrace). Designer suits, expensive watches, and tasteful jewelry. Every detail meticulously groomed and calculated for maximum professional impact. Commanding presence with Ivy League polish. Cold, calculating eyes that assess value in everything. Moves with the confidence of old money despite his mortal origins. Conservative but expensive style—Brooks Brothers, bespoke tailoring, Italian leather shoes. Nothing flashy, everything quality.
concept: Vulture finance specialist

name: Marisol "Roadrunner" Vega
notes: Knows Garou patrol patterns anecdotally; leaves coterie sigils on mile markers. Coterie member of Valley Watch. Has allies including Rafael "Switchback" Ortiz (Brujah sparring partner) and Tahlia Vance (Toreador coterie-mate who bankrolls gear). Maintains haven in abandoned maintenance shed off I-17 with buried cache and hidden culvert entrance.
biography: Former long-haul driver on the Vegas–Phoenix route. Embraced at a rest stop by an independent Gangrel. Now serves as tracker and hunter for the Valley Watch coterie, mapping the supernatural 'safe trails' around the Valley and keeping the coterie alive. She can cross the wastelands around Phoenix without vehicles and reads sign like scripture. Her goal is to map the supernatural 'safe trails' around the Valley and keep the coterie alive.
appearance: Lean, sunburnt Latina in a faded trucker jacket; dust-caked boots; hawk-bright eyes.
concept: Desert tracker and coterie scout

name: Roxanne Murphy
notes: High priestess of The Hole; maintains layered security and monthly Akhu rites in a hidden shrine dedicated to Set.
biography: After years operating in smaller occult circles, Roxanne established herself in Mesa by building The Hole as both a profitable nightlife destination and a perfectly concealed Setite temple. She rules its levels like a descending mystery cult: public pleasure above, true corruption and worship below. She works closely with Marcus (operations) and Jennifer (security) and maintains the blasphemy-shrine where she performs Akhu rites. She is aware of Camarilla eyes on her domain but is confident in her layered defenses.
appearance: Impeccably composed Setite woman who favors Egyptian-inspired jewelry and rich fabrics. Often seen in the private levels of The Hole, lit by warm, low light that accents her ritual regalia.
concept: Setite temple-mistress running a nightclub front in Mesa
